title: meconopsis speciosa subsp. speciosa
intro: Meconopsis speciosa subsp. speciosa, also known as the Himalayan blue poppy, is a flowering plant species native to the Himalayas. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Meconopsis speciosa subsp. speciosa can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and in some regions, it is also used in traditional cooking.
